steps.title: Find the vertex and determine if it is a maximum or minimum
steps.content:
The equation is in vertex form $$y = a(x-h)^2 + k$$ with $$a = -16$$, $$h = 7.2$$, and $$k = 829$$.

Thus the vertex is $$(h,k) = (7.2,829)$$. Because $$a \lt 0$$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex represents the maximum height.

Hence the maximum height is $$k = 829$$ feet and it occurs at time $$x = h = 7.2$$ seconds.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex coordinates as time and maximum height
steps.content: In this context, $$x$$ measures time in seconds after launch and $$y$$ measures height in feet. Therefore the firework reaches its maximum height of 829 feet at 7.2 seconds after launch. That matches the correct answer; C is correct.
